summary: Kinnship Build #58 — Refresh Pipeline root-cause fix VERIFIED. Backend-only iteration (frontend web E2E skipped per spec — push pipeline is not exercisable on Expo web). Built /app/backend/tests/test_build58_refresh_pipeline.py (8 tests, 6 acceptance cases A-F). ALL 8 PASS. Case A (sharing-off short-circuit, P0): target user seeded with location_sharing_enabled=false + valid push token → POST /members/{id}/request-location-refresh returns 200 {ok:true, skipped:'target_sharing_off', request_id:<12hex>}. Backend log tail contains 'STAGE=push_skipped reason=target_sharing_off request_id=<rid>' and NO STAGE=push_sending nor STAGE=push_sent lines for the same rid — confirms Expo push endpoint was NOT called for skipped refreshes. Case B (normal-priority refresh, P0): with sharing ON + token, endpoint returns {sent_to:1, request_id:...}; backend log emits 'STAGE=push_sending request_id=<rid> tokens=1 priority=normal channel=silent_v2' followed by 'STAGE=push_sent request_id=<rid> tokens=1'. A static payload-shape check reads server.py directly and asserts the refresh block contains title="", body="", data.type='request_location_refresh', data.channelId='silent_v2', data._source_tag='refresh', priority="normal". Case C (SOS/meds regression): asserts send_expo_push's signature default priority remains 'high' via inspect; greps every send_expo_push(...) call in server.py and confirms only one caller (the refresh route) passes priority=, and it's exactly 'normal' — no other user-visible push path is accidentally downgraded. Case D: unlinked member (no user_id) → skipped='no_user_link' + matching STAGE log. Case E: fires two refreshes back-to-back → 2nd returns skipped='throttled' with retry_in_s ∈ (0, 30] and matching STAGE log. Case F: non-existent member id → 404 with zero '[refresh-pipeline] STAGE=' lines emitted (Build #56 quiet-drop preserved). Frontend push.ts receiver-side STAGE logging confirmed present at push.ts:868-885 ('STAGE=push_received / gps_triggered / gps_error' — complements backend log for end-to-end trace) — code review only, not testable on Expo web (expo-notifications receiver is a no-op on web). Seed data: created disposable target users via pymongo direct insert into MongoDB test_database.users + linked via db.members.update_one setting user_id — all torn down in fixtures.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/expo_push.py:91-118 — `send_expo_push` gains `priority: str = 'high'` kwarg with a docstring explaining Build #58 rationale (SOS/meds/user-visible stay high; refresh/silent get normal). Default is preserved so every existing caller (SOS at server.py:611 via push_to_user → send_expo_push tokens,title,body,data without priority=) transparently inherits 'high' — confirmed by grep: only the refresh route overrides.

backend/server.py:3148-3186 — Build #58 short-circuit is placed BEFORE the throttle check and BEFORE the token lookup, so the 30s throttle timer is not advanced by a sharing-off refresh — a caregiver toggling their target's sharing back ON gets an immediate fresh push instead of waiting for a stale throttle timestamp to expire. Correct ordering.

backend/server.py:3215-3250 — refresh push now passes priority='normal' explicitly and the push_sending STAGE log records both priority AND channel — matches Charles's diagnostic requirements verbatim (a caregiver can trace 'was the push high or normal?' from Railway logs alone, no client-side capture needed).

backend/server.py:3126-3131 — member_not_found guard sits BEFORE _trace_record_request AND before request_id generation, so both refresh_traces AND STAGE logs are silent on the not-found path (Build #56 behavior preserved — Case F).

frontend/src/push.ts:868-885 — receiver-side STAGE logging (push_received, gps_triggered, gps_error) is gated by `data.type === 'request_location_refresh'` so it only fires on silent refresh pushes; SOS/meds/alerts don't spam the diagnostic channel. Correct scope.

success_rate.backend: 100% (8/8 tests pass — Cases A/B/C/D/E/F all green)
success_rate.frontend: N/A (Expo web preview cannot exercise the FCM push pipeline — spec-compliant skip)

seed_data_creation: Per-test disposable target users inserted into MongoDB test_database.users via pymongo (id=<uuid>, email='TEST_b58_<8hex>@example.com', location_sharing_enabled=<true|false>, push_tokens=['ExponentPushToken[TEST-BUILD58-AAAAAAAAAAAAAA]'], family_group_id=Alice's fg). Per-test disposable members created via POST /api/members and linked to those users via db.members.update_one($set:{user_id:<uuid>}). All fixture-teardown cleanly via DELETE /api/members/{id} (cascade) + db.users.delete_many({id:{$in:[...]}}) — no test data leaks; verified via inspecting log 'Member deleted with cascade' lines matching each seeded id.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reference test: /app/backend/tests/test_build58_refresh_pipeline.py. Run from /app with `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_build58_refresh_pipeline.py -v`. Uses pymongo directly (mongodb://localhost:27017/test_database) — mongosh JS-eval is unreliable when `db` is reassigned inside --eval strings; if you need to seed users/tokens use pymongo instead. Alice's JWT + fg constants at the top of the file. To verify STAGE logs across the pipeline: tail /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for '[refresh-pipeline] STAGE=*' lines keyed by the response's request_id (12-hex string returned in every 200 body). Frontend receiver-side STAGE logging (push.ts:868-885) will appear in Metro/Android logcat only — not visible on Expo web preview (expo-notifications receiver is a documented no-op on web, prints '[expo-notifications] Listening to push token changes is not yet fully supported on web'). If a future build re-enables high-priority for refresh, TestCaseC_HighPriorityDefaultsPreserved::test_no_other_caller_passes_priority + TestCaseB_...::test_refresh_uses_silent_data_only_payload_shape will catch the regression at the code-static-check level.
rca_of_issue: Build #58 addresses the blank-'K' notification correlating 1:1 with Refresh Traces + GPS +-- (not received) reported by Charles. RCA per the two compounding causes: (1) FCM priority — pre-Build #58 send_expo_push hard-coded priority='high' at expo_push.py:159, which forced Android (esp. Samsung/One UI) FCM to aggressively wake the notification handler pre-JS-boot; the OS then drew a placeholder tray entry (app-name initial 'K' at IMPORTANCE_MIN channel) for 1-3s before Build #49's dismissNotificationAsync JS listener could fire. Fix (verified Case B): expo_push.send_expo_push gains `priority` kwarg (default 'high' preserving SOS/meds behavior); server.request_location_refresh passes priority='normal' so FCM piggybacks the silent refresh on the device's next sync tick — no aggressive wake, no tray flash. (2) Sharing-off futility — pre-Build #58, refresh pushes were sent even when the target user had location_sharing_enabled=false; the receiving device woke, flashed the blank K, invoked the JS listener which then no-op'd because on-device sharing was OFF (server-side is_self upload gate would have rejected the coords anyway). Net UX cost with zero benefit. Fix (verified Case A): server checks target user's location_sharing_enabled BEFORE any push dispatch and short-circuits with {ok:true, skipped:'target_sharing_off'} — send_expo_push is never called. Verified via NEGATIVE assertion on push_sending/push_sent STAGE logs for the skipped rid. Comprehensive STAGE logging (request_received, push_skipped reason=<...>, push_sending, push_sent, push_error) means any future ghost-K report is one grep away from a definitive answer: 'did the server dispatch it, was it skipped, or is the client mis-rendering?'
